records.ety: [L. capax, -acis, fr. capere to take. See Heave.]

defs: Having capacity; able to contain much; large; roomy; spacious; extended; broad; as, a capacious vessel, room, bay, or harbor.
quotes: In the capacious recesses of his mind.

defs: Able or qualified to make large views of things, as in obtaining knowledge or forming designs; comprehensive; liberal.
